title: Email Triage Daemon Contract
description: Contract between the arkai-gmail triage daemon and the OpenClaw agent. ADR-0002 Option C: daemon runs on host, writes JSON to workspace/input/emails/, agent reads via safe_fs_read_text.

entrypoint.note: One-shot script, NOT a persistent daemon. Run via launchd/cron on schedule.
entrypoint.canonical_launch_note: All logging goes to stderr (launchd StandardErrorPath). Stdout is clean. Exit 0 = success, exit 1 = fatal.

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.description: Anthropic API key for Reader LLM (Sonnet classification)

GMAIL_OUTPUT_DIR.description: Directory to write JSON result files
GMAIL_OUTPUT_DIR.note: Must be readable by OpenClaw container (Docker volume mount)

GMAIL_CONFIG_DIR.description: arkai-gmail config directory (OAuth tokens, history ID)

GMAIL_TRIAGE_LIMIT.description: Maximum emails to process per run

GMAIL_GATE_CONFIG.description: Path to gates.yaml config for pre-AI filtering

GMAIL_RETAIN_DAYS.description: Delete output JSON files older than N days. Prevents unbounded growth.
GMAIL_RETAIN_DAYS.note: Set to 0 to disable retention cleanup. Only deletes files matching {12-hex}.json pattern.

output_directory.idempotency: Same email always produces same filename. Re-runs overwrite, not duplicate.

failure_semantics.per_email: Log error to stderr, skip failed email, continue pipeline
failure_semantics.fatal: Exit code 1 for auth failure, missing API key, or config issues
failure_semantics.success: Exit code 0 even if 0 emails were processed (no new mail is not an error)
failure_semantics.note: Idempotent — safe to re-run. Gmail history ID tracks what was already fetched.

retention.behavior: After each triage run, delete *.json files in output_dir older than retain_days. Only deletes files matching /^[a-f0-9]{12}\.json$/ pattern (won't touch other files).

scheduling.concurrency: Single instance only. No locking needed — Gmail history ID prevents duplicates.
scheduling.note: Each run takes ~3-8s per email (LLM API call). 10 emails ≈ 30-80s.

prerequisites.gmail_auth: Must run 'arkai-gmail auth' once to complete OAuth flow
prerequisites.python_venv: Uses ~/AI/arkai-gmail/.venv/bin/python3 — NOT system Python. All deps pre-installed.

oauth_scope_note.why_not_reduced_yet: Reducing scope would break arkai-gmail CLI --execute mode (labeling, archiving). Scope reduction deferred until daemon and CLI auth are split.
oauth_scope_note.security_mitigation: Token stays on host at ~/.arkai-gmail/token.json. Never enters Docker container (ADR-0002).

output_schema.description: One JSON file per classified email. This is the exact schema of each output file.

processed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this email was processed

classification.description: Reader LLM classification (null if gate skipped)

proposed_actions.description: Actions proposed by Reader, validated by Critic

id.description: 12-char SHA256 triage ID (also the filename stem)

labels.description: Gmail label IDs at time of processing

flagged.description: True if suspicious patterns were detected

validation.classify_only: Daemon imports GmailIngestion, PreAIGate, ReaderLLM, Critic only. Actor is NOT imported. No Gmail write API calls exist in triage_daemon.py. Verified: `grep -c 'actor\|execute\|modify\|label\|archive' scripts/triage_daemon.py` returns 0 hits on Gmail API write methods.
validation.retention: Tested: fresh file (mtime=now) survives, old file (mtime=30d ago) deleted, non-matching file (readme.txt) untouched. Pattern: /^[a-f0-9]{12}\.json$/. Default: 7 days.
validation.output_roundtrip: DaemonTriageResult → model_dump_json() → model_validate_json() round-trip verified. All fields survive serialization.

classification.summary: Boss requesting Q1 analysis by Friday
classification.reasoning: Direct request from manager with clear deadline
